title: Acetosella refracta
intro: Acetosella refracta, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the oxalis genus. The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of gastrointestinal issues due to its antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to help manage diabetes and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Acetosella refracta include oxalic acid, malic acid, and anthraquinone glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Wood Sorrel include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support digestive health, lower blood sugar levels, and reduce inflammation.
